documents.title: Ministry of Higher Education Compliance — Official
documents.summary: Omani government scholarship recipients must maintain compliance with both their host country's immigration regulations AND MoHE-specific requirements. Failure to meet MoHE obligations may result in scholarship termination, repayment demands, or restrictions on future government employment.

scope.failureImpact: Scholarship revocation, financial liability for all funds received, ban from future government employment, potential legal action in Oman

corePrinciples: Academic Excellence Principle: Maintain minimum GPA requirements
Program Stability Principle: No unauthorized program changes
Financial Accountability Principle: All funds used for approved purposes
Reporting Principle: Timely submission of all required documents
Return Service Principle: Fulfill post-completion service obligation

details: Minimum GPA: 3.0/4.0 or equivalent (B average) for graduate students; 2.5/4.0 for undergraduate
Must maintain full-time enrollment each semester (verified by DSO)
No academic probation beyond one semester without MoHE approval
All grade reports must be submitted to MoHE through official channels each term

commonViolations.text: GPA falling below minimum requirement for two consecutive terms
Failure to submit academic reports on time

mitigation: Utilize university tutoring and academic support services
Report academic difficulties to MoHE liaison early

details: Major/degree program changes require PRIOR written approval from MoHE
University transfers require 30-day advance notice and justification
Extension requests must demonstrate compelling academic/medical reasons
Early graduation must be reported within 10 days of degree conferral
controls: Submit program change requests 60 days in advance
Maintain communication with MoHE liaison officer
Document all academic decisions affecting program duration

details: Mandatory return to Oman within 60 days of program completion
Minimum 5-year employment in Omani public sector (may vary by specialization)
Failure to return triggers full scholarship repayment plus 20% penalty
Extension requests for further study require MoHE approval before current program completion

commonViolations.text: Remaining in U.S. beyond 60-day return window
Accepting private sector employment in Oman without MoHE approval

mitigation: Begin job search in Oman 6 months before graduation
Coordinate with MoHE placement office for public sector positions
Document all job applications and interviews

MoHE_Scholarship_Office.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education
MoHE_Scholarship_Office.title: Scholarship Department
MoHE_Scholarship_Office.phone_note: Phone number not verified. Use the official MoHE portal or email to reach the Scholarship Department.

links.title: Omani Ministry of Higher Education
links.description: Official MoHE portal. Navigate to the Scholarships section for student-specific guidance.

links.title: Embassy of Oman in Washington D.C.
links.description: Contact the Cultural Attaché office for student affairs assistance.

complianceNotes: All MoHE communications must be in Arabic or formal English
Keep copies of all correspondence for minimum 5 years
Report any legal or immigration issues to MoHE within 24 hours
Contact MoHE directly to confirm which personal decisions require prior approval — requirements vary by scholarship cohort and agreement year.
Annual physical presence verification required during summer breaks

documents.title: MoHE Scholarship Financial Entitlements and Management
documents.summary: MoHE scholarships cover tuition, living allowance, and approved incidentals. Specific amounts and entitlements are defined in your individual scholarship agreement and vary by destination country, degree level, and scholarship cohort year. This document covers the standard entitlement categories and procedures for claims, stipend management, and emergency financial assistance.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients — US, UK, and Australia
scope.failureImpact: Stipend suspension for non-compliance; financial liability for unauthorized expenditure; scholarship termination for financial misconduct

details: Tuition and mandatory fees: paid directly by MoHE to your institution in most cases — do not pay tuition out of pocket; if you are invoiced directly by your institution, contact MoHE Scholarship Department immediately before paying

Monthly living allowance (stipend): transferred to your designated host-country bank account — the exact amount is specified in your scholarship agreement and varies by destination and degree level; confirm your entitlement in your agreement

Annual return airfare: economy class round-trip between Oman and your study city, typically once per academic year — the booking and reimbursement process varies; confirm with MoHE before purchasing any tickets

Health insurance (OSHC/NHS Surcharge/equivalent): typically paid directly by MoHE or reimbursed upon submission of proof — confirm your specific arrangement before purchasing health cover independently to avoid paying twice
Initial settlement allowance: a one-time payment to cover arrival costs in your first semester — confirm eligibility and amount in your scholarship agreement
Equipment allowance: a one-time laptop or equipment allowance may be available — confirm eligibility, amount, and pre-approval requirements with MoHE before purchasing
controls: Never spend money assuming MoHE will reimburse it without written prior authorization — confirm all claims before incurring the expense
Keep all receipts, invoices, and booking confirmations for any expense you intend to claim from MoHE

details: Your stipend is typically paid monthly; timing (advance or arrears) is defined in your scholarship agreement
You must provide MoHE with your current host-country bank account BSB/sort code/routing number and account number — update this promptly whenever you change banks
Stipend may be suspended if: your academic report is overdue, you are on academic probation, MoHE cannot verify your enrollment status, or a compliance issue is under investigation

If your stipend is late or suspended, email MoHE Scholarship Department at scholarships@mohe.gov.om immediately — include your student ID, scholarship year, host country, and the affected payment period
MoHE does not advance payments against future months — manage your monthly budget accordingly
For approved reimbursable expenses (medical, airfare, equipment), submit claims through the official MoHE portal (mohe.gov.om) or by email to scholarships@mohe.gov.om
Claims must be submitted within 90 days of incurring the expense unless your agreement specifies a different window
Required documentation for each claim: original receipts or invoices, your bank account details, your student ID, and a brief written explanation of the expense
Processing time: allow 4–6 weeks for routine reimbursements; for urgent medical claims, clearly state the urgency in your submission
Claims submitted without complete documentation are returned without processing — do not discard any receipts related to your scholarship activities
For a genuine financial emergency (medical crisis, theft, natural disaster), contact MoHE Scholarship Department by email and phone on the same day
Submit a written request describing: the nature of the emergency, the amount required, how it will be used, and supporting documentation
Emergency assistance is assessed case-by-case and may be treated as an advance deductible from future stipend payments
Also contact the Omani Cultural Attaché or Embassy in your host country immediately — they can provide in-country support and may be able to accelerate the Muscat response
All scholarship funds must be used for their approved purpose — using tuition or equipment funds for personal expenses is a scholarship violation
Working beyond your visa's permitted work hours to supplement income without MoHE authorization is both a visa violation and a scholarship violation
MoHE may conduct periodic financial audits — maintain records of all significant expenses throughout your studies
Misrepresentation in financial claims (inflated receipts, false invoices) is grounds for immediate scholarship termination and potential legal action in Oman

MoHE_Scholarship_Finance.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education — Scholarship Department
MoHE_Scholarship_Finance.note: Include your student ID, scholarship year, and host country in all financial correspondence

links.title: Omani Ministry of Higher Education
links.description: Official MoHE portal — navigate to Scholarships for financial guidance and forms

complianceNotes: Your scholarship agreement is the authoritative source for your specific entitlements — the amounts in this document are general guidance only
Never purchase airfare or equipment expecting reimbursement without written pre-authorization from MoHE
Report any stipend payment failure immediately — delays compound and are difficult to resolve retrospectively

documents.title: MoHE Reporting Requirements, Leave of Absence, and Study Extensions
documents.summary: MoHE scholarship recipients have mandatory reporting obligations throughout their study period. Failure to submit required reports on time results in immediate stipend suspension. Leaves of absence and study extensions require advance written approval from MoHE — unauthorized extensions receive no financial support and place the scholarship in breach. All changes to your academic program must be disclosed to MoHE before they take effect.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients — US, UK, and Australia
scope.failureImpact: Stipend suspension, scholarship termination, repayment demand for unauthorized extension periods

details: Academic Progress Report (each semester): submit to MoHE at the end of every semester — must include your official transcript or grade report issued by your institution's registrar
Enrollment Verification (each term): confirm active full-time enrollment at the start of each academic term — typically a letter from your university's international student office or registrar
Supervisor / Advisor Report (PhD and research students only): a signed progress report from your primary supervisor is required each semester in addition to transcripts

Graduation Notification: notify MoHE within 10 days of your degree being conferred — submit your degree certificate and official final transcript; continued stipend payments after graduation must be repaid
Address and Contact Updates: notify MoHE of any change to your residential address, phone number, or email address within 14 days of the change

Annual Presence Verification: some scholarship agreements require physical sign-in at your nearest Omani embassy or Cultural Attaché office during the annual summer break — check your specific agreement for this requirement

commonViolations.text: Submitting academic progress report more than 30 days after semester end
commonViolations.consequence: Stipend suspended until the report is received and reviewed

commonViolations.text: Failing to notify MoHE of graduation within 10 days
commonViolations.consequence: Continued stipend payments that must be repaid; delay in initiating the return-obligation process

controls: Set calendar reminders at the end of each semester to prepare and submit your academic report within the required window
Request your official transcript from the registrar immediately after grades are released — do not wait
Keep copies of every submission to MoHE, including email delivery confirmations

details: Annual Leave: MoHE grants annual leave entitlement to return to Oman during semester breaks — confirm the duration and scheduling rules in your scholarship agreement; coordinate timing with your university's academic calendar

Sick Leave: for illness requiring 2 or more consecutive weeks away from your studies, submit a formal sick leave request to MoHE with a medical certificate from a licensed physician; extended sick leave may require specialist assessment documentation

Emergency Leave: for immediate family emergencies (death of an immediate family member, life-threatening illness of a parent), contact MoHE by email and phone on the same day; submit supporting documentation upon returning to your studies

Maternity / Paternity Leave: available under most scholarship agreements — contact MoHE Scholarship Department for current duration and documentation requirements; apply at least 30 days before expected leave where possible
Leave does not automatically extend your scholarship end date — clarify the impact on your program completion timeline in writing at the time of your leave approval
Your host-country visa may be affected by extended leave — understand the visa implications before departing (see the relevant country KB entry for your destination)
controls: Always apply for leave in writing and receive written approval before beginning any leave period
File all leave approvals in a dedicated folder — you may need to reference them at return-obligation processing time

details: Your scholarship covers the standard program duration — extensions require prior written approval from MoHE before your current scholarship end date

Grounds for extension: academic reasons (course requirements changed, failed subject requiring repeat), medical circumstances with documentation, supervisor departure or change (research students), or force majeure

How to apply: email scholarships@mohe.gov.om at least 60 days before your current scholarship end date; include — reason for extension, supporting documentation (medical certificate, university letter, supervisor statement), expected new completion date
MoHE may approve a shorter extension than requested — do not assume the full requested period will be granted
Your host-country visa must also be extended separately — MoHE approval does not extend your visa; begin the visa extension process before your current visa expires
Stipend continues during an approved extension period only; remaining enrolled beyond your scholarship end date without approval means no financial support and a scholarship breach

commonViolations.text: Remaining enrolled beyond scholarship end date without MoHE approval
commonViolations.consequence: No financial support; scholarship in breach; potential repayment demand for the unauthorized period

commonViolations.text: Applying for extension after the scholarship end date has already passed
commonViolations.consequence: Application likely refused; student is already in unauthorized extension status

details: Any arrest, formal legal charge, or immigration violation must be reported to MoHE within 24 hours
Include in your report: a factual description of what occurred, the date and location, the authorities involved, and any immediate consequences or charges
Contact the Omani Cultural Attaché or Embassy in your host country simultaneously — they can provide consular assistance and communicate with MoHE on your behalf
Failure to report within 24 hours is treated as a separate scholarship violation, compounding the original issue

MoHE_Scholarship_Office.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education — Scholarship Department

links.title: Omani Ministry of Higher Education
links.description: Official MoHE portal for scholarship forms and reporting submissions

forms: MoHE Academic Progress Report Form (each semester)

complianceNotes: A late academic report immediately suspends your stipend — submit early, never late
Leave periods that extend into a teaching semester must be formally approved; a break that runs longer than expected is not automatically covered by your initial leave approval
Always obtain written confirmation from MoHE for every leave and extension approval — verbal confirmations or WhatsApp messages are not sufficient for formal scholarship records

documents.title: MoHE Enrollment and Course Load Requirements
documents.summary: MoHE requires all scholarship recipients to maintain full-time enrollment throughout their program. 'Full-time' has a specific credit hour definition that varies by degree level and institution. Dropping below full-time — even temporarily — constitutes a scholarship violation unless MoHE has granted prior written approval. This document covers credit hour minimums, course withdrawal rules, summer enrollment, failed courses, and academic probation procedures.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients — US, UK, and Australia
scope.failureImpact: Stipend suspension; scholarship placed in breach; potential repayment demand for semesters spent below full-time without approval

details: Undergraduate (US): minimum 12 credit hours per semester is the federal standard for full-time status — confirm with your institution's registrar as some universities set 15 credits as full-time for scholarship purposes
Graduate / Master's (US): typically 9 credit hours per semester constitutes full-time at most US universities — confirm your institution's definition as it varies
PhD / Doctoral (US): varies significantly — some programs define full-time as 9 credits, others as dissertation registration units only; confirm with your DSO and registrar in writing
UK (undergraduate and postgraduate): full-time is defined by your institution's term structure — typically 120 credits per academic year (60 per semester); confirm with your Student Records office
Australia: full-time is typically 0.5 EFTSL per semester (2 standard units of study) — confirm with your institution as definitions vary by faculty and course type
MoHE defers to your institution's registrar definition of full-time — obtain written confirmation of your status each semester and keep it on file
controls: Request a written enrollment verification letter from your registrar at the start of each semester confirming full-time status
Submit this letter to MoHE as part of your semester reporting obligation

details: Dropping a course that takes you below full-time status requires PRIOR written approval from MoHE — do not drop first and notify later
Withdrawing from a full course load (all courses) in a semester is treated as a Leave of Absence and requires formal MoHE approval (see MOHE-REPORTING-LEAVE-2026)
Late withdrawals that appear as 'W' on your transcript must be reported to MoHE — they do not count toward GPA but do affect enrollment status records
Medical withdrawals must be supported by a physician's certificate and reported to MoHE within 14 days — they are treated as medical leave and may be approved retroactively in genuine cases

commonViolations.text: Dropping a course without MoHE approval, resulting in below-full-time status
commonViolations.consequence: Stipend suspended for the affected semester; scholarship placed under review

commonViolations.text: Withdrawing from all courses without formal leave approval
commonViolations.consequence: Treated as unauthorized leave; scholarship may be terminated

details: Summer enrollment is generally not required unless your program has mandatory summer components or you are on an approved remediation plan
If you enroll in summer courses, you remain subject to all MoHE reporting and conduct obligations during that period
Stipend payments during summer vary by scholarship agreement — some agreements suspend the stipend over summer if you return to Oman; confirm your specific arrangement
PhD students in continuous research programs are typically considered enrolled year-round — confirm your enrollment status with your DSO or supervisor during summer
Failing a course must be reported in your next Academic Progress Report — do not omit failed grades from submissions
A failing grade that does not itself breach the GPA minimum must still be disclosed — MoHE monitors trends, not just thresholds
Repeating a failed course is permitted without MoHE approval if it does not extend your program beyond the approved scholarship end date
If a course repeat will cause you to exceed your approved program duration, you must apply for a Study Extension before your scholarship end date (see MOHE-REPORTING-LEAVE-2026)

Two or more failed courses in a single semester triggers an automatic MoHE academic review — proactively contact the Scholarship Department with an explanation and remediation plan before they contact you
controls: If you fail a course, email MoHE proactively with an explanation and your remediation plan before submitting the formal progress report
Attach your academic advisor's statement confirming your continuation plan

details: If your university places you on academic probation, notify MoHE in writing within 14 days — do not wait for the next scheduled report
MoHE permits one semester of academic probation with a documented remediation plan; a second consecutive probation semester triggers scholarship review
You must submit a MoHE-approved Academic Improvement Plan (AIP) within 30 days of being placed on probation — the AIP must be countersigned by your academic advisor
Clearing academic probation must be confirmed by a letter from your registrar or academic advisor and submitted to MoHE promptly
Being dismissed from your program due to academic failure is grounds for immediate scholarship termination and full repayment demand

commonViolations.text: Failing to notify MoHE of academic probation within the 14-day window
commonViolations.consequence: Treated as a reporting violation compounding the academic issue; stipend may be suspended

controls: If you are at risk of academic probation, contact MoHE before the probation is formally issued — early disclosure is treated more favorably than post-facto disclosure

MoHE_Scholarship_Office.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education — Scholarship Department

forms: Enrollment Verification Letter (from your institution's registrar — each semester)
Course Withdrawal / Drop Approval Request (submit to MoHE before dropping)

complianceNotes: Never assume that dropping a course is a minor administrative matter — if it affects your full-time status, it requires MoHE approval first
Your DSO (US) / Student Records office (UK/AU) and MoHE must both be informed of enrollment changes — informing one does not satisfy the other
When in doubt about whether a change affects your full-time status, email MoHE before making it — a 24-hour wait for confirmation is far less costly than a stipend suspension

documents.title: MoHE Work Authorization and Employment Rules
documents.summary: MoHE scholarship recipients are primarily funded to study, not to work. Employment is restricted under both your host-country visa and your MoHE scholarship agreement. Violating MoHE employment rules — even when your visa technically permits some work — can result in stipend reduction, scholarship review, or termination. This document covers what work is permitted, what requires MoHE pre-approval, how income affects your stipend, and how to handle research assistantships and internships.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients — US, UK, and Australia
scope.failureImpact: Stipend suspension or reduction; scholarship termination for unauthorized employment; visa violation if work exceeds visa permit

details: Your MoHE scholarship is a full financial support package — MoHE's position is that you should not need to work to support yourself during your studies
The default position under most scholarship agreements is that paid employment requires prior written MoHE authorization, regardless of whether your visa permits it
Verify your specific scholarship agreement — some agreements prohibit all paid work; others permit limited on-campus work without prior approval; the agreement text is the authority
MoHE and your host-country visa rules are independent obligations — satisfying one does not satisfy the other; you must comply with both simultaneously
Research Assistantships (RA) and Teaching Assistantships (TA) offered by your university are the most commonly permitted form of employment for MoHE scholars

Many scholarship agreements explicitly permit RA/TA positions that are part of your academic program, subject to the following conditions: the position is offered by your enrolled institution; the work is directly related to your field of study; the hours do not interfere with your full-time enrollment; and the position complies with your visa's work authorization rules
Before accepting any RA or TA offer, email MoHE with the position details (role, hours per week, compensation, and how it relates to your studies) and wait for written confirmation
RA/TA income received as a tuition waiver rather than a cash stipend is generally less likely to be treated as a scholarship conflict — but still disclose it to MoHE
If your RA/TA stipend is significant, MoHE may adjust your living allowance accordingly — disclose proactively rather than risk a later audit
controls: Do not sign an RA/TA contract or begin work before receiving written MoHE confirmation
Keep copies of all RA/TA contracts and MoHE authorization correspondence

details: Curricular Practical Training (CPT) in the US, placement years in the UK, and Work Integrated Learning (WIL) in Australia — when these are a mandatory or credit-bearing component of your degree — are generally permissible
Optional or elective internships that are not required for graduation require prior MoHE approval even if your visa permits them
For CPT (US): your DSO must authorize CPT on your I-20 before you begin; ensure MoHE is also informed in writing
Paid internships: disclose compensation details to MoHE; MoHE may request that stipend payments be paused or reduced during a paid internship period
Unpaid internships that are part of your academic program and do not conflict with enrollment are generally low-risk but should still be disclosed
authorizationSources: Your institution's DSO or Career Services office

details: Off-campus employment not authorized by your visa is strictly prohibited and is simultaneously a visa violation and a scholarship violation
Freelance, consulting, or self-employment income earned in your host country without prior MoHE and visa authorization is prohibited
Working for a company or individual in Oman (remotely) while on scholarship may conflict with your scholarship agreement — confirm with MoHE before accepting any remote Omani employment
Social media monetization, sponsored content, or any activity generating income that is not disclosed to MoHE is a scholarship risk — disclose any income source regardless of amount
Working more hours than your visa permits — even in an otherwise authorized position — is both a visa and scholarship violation

commonViolations.text: Taking undisclosed off-campus part-time work to supplement income
commonViolations.consequence: Scholarship termination; visa revocation; repayment demand for all scholarship funds received

commonViolations.text: Accepting a paid internship without MoHE approval

details: MoHE may reduce your living allowance stipend if you are earning income that covers your living costs — the scholarship is designed to fund your studies, not supplement employment income
You are required to disclose any employment income to MoHE in your regular reporting — treat this the same as the GPA and enrollment reporting obligation
Do not assume that because MoHE has not yet adjusted your stipend after income disclosure, that adjustment will not come retroactively — MoHE may recalculate and deduct from future payments
Tax obligations on employment income are your personal responsibility — MoHE does not advise on tax and your income may be taxable in your host country; consult a tax advisor

MoHE_Scholarship_Office.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education — Scholarship Department

forms: Employment Authorization Request (submit to MoHE before accepting any position)

complianceNotes: The safest rule: disclose any opportunity to MoHE before accepting — not after
Visa work authorization and MoHE work authorization are separate — having one does not mean you have the other
MoHE audits are retrospective — unauthorized income discovered months later still triggers repayment and scholarship action

documents.title: MoHE Pre-Departure Requirements and First-Arrival Checklist
documents.summary: MoHE scholarship recipients must complete a set of requirements before departing Oman and within the first 30–60 days of arriving in their host country. Missing these steps causes delays to stipend payments, visa complications, and compliance flags that are difficult to resolve retroactively. This document covers what MoHE requires before you travel, what to complete immediately on arrival, and the key institutions you must register with in your first weeks abroad.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients — US, UK, and Australia — at the start of their scholarship
scope.failureImpact: Delayed or withheld stipend payments; visa complications; compliance record flags that follow you through your scholarship period

details: Obtain your MoHE departure authorization — some scholarship agreements require written MoHE clearance before you book travel; check your agreement
Attend the MoHE pre-departure orientation if one is scheduled for your cohort year — attendance is mandatory and your scholarship contact will notify you of the date
Ensure your passport validity covers at least 6 months beyond your expected program end date — renew in Oman before departure if needed; renewing abroad is significantly more complex

Obtain your host-country student visa before departing — do not travel on a tourist visa or visa waiver with the intent to convert it to a student visa, as this creates immigration complications in all three destination countries

Complete your medical examination if required by your host-country visa (required for Australia student visa; not required for US F-1 or UK Student visa at time of writing — verify current requirements)
Register your bank account details with MoHE before departure so your stipend can be set up — delays in bank registration are the most common cause of missed first stipend payments

Obtain copies of your full scholarship agreement, MoHE letter of sponsorship, and all financial entitlement documentation — carry these in both print and digital form; you will need them at your university, your bank, and potentially at immigration
controls: Create a physical and digital folder with: passport, visa, scholarship agreement, MoHE sponsorship letter, university acceptance letter, and health insurance documents
Do not book flights until you have received your student visa — book only after visa approval

details: Port of entry: have your student visa, university acceptance letter, and MoHE sponsorship letter accessible — immigration officers may ask to see evidence of funding
US (F-1 visa): your SEVIS record must be activated within 30 days of your program start date — report to your DSO within 3 business days of arrival to complete I-20 check-in
UK (Student visa): report to your university's International Student Advisory Service within 3 days of arrival — your CAS number must be confirmed active

Australia (Student visa 500): report to your university's international student office within 5 business days of arrival — your CoE (Confirmation of Enrolment) must be active and your OSHC cover must begin

Contact the Omani Cultural Attaché or Embassy in your host country within your first week to register as an Omani scholar — this registration is separate from MoHE reporting and gives you access to in-country consular support
Locate the nearest urgent medical facility, your university health center, and the Omani embassy address before you need them — not during an emergency
authorizationSources: Omani Embassy in your host country

details: Open a local bank account and register the account details with MoHE immediately — stipend payments cannot be processed without a verified host-country bank account
Confirm your enrollment and full-time status with your university registrar and obtain a written enrollment verification letter — submit this to MoHE as your initial enrollment confirmation
Obtain your university student ID, library access, and university email — these are required for ongoing academic reporting and some MoHE correspondence

Arrange health insurance: confirm whether MoHE is paying directly or reimbursing you — do not assume coverage has started; verify with your institution that you are covered from day one (especially in Australia where OSHC must be active from arrival)
Set up your MoHE online reporting account if you have not already done so — confirm your login credentials before your first report is due
Provide your full host-country address and local phone number to MoHE in writing within 14 days of arrival
controls: Keep a checklist of completed items with the date each was done — you may be asked to confirm completion dates by MoHE during your first review
Send a single arrival confirmation email to MoHE within 7 days of arrival: include your local address, phone number, institution name, program, and bank account registration status

details: Submit your initial Academic Enrollment Confirmation to MoHE — this is a formal letter from your registrar confirming your program, full-time status, and expected completion date
Confirm your stipend has been received — if your first stipend payment has not arrived within 30 days of your confirmed bank account registration, contact MoHE Scholarship Department immediately

US only: complete your SEVIS check-in and verify your I-94 record online (cbp.gov/i94) — your I-94 admission class must show F-1 or J-1 (not WT/WB); contact your DSO immediately if there is a discrepancy
Australia only: confirm your OSHC health cover is active and that you have your membership card and policy number — log in to your OSHC provider portal to verify

Identify your university's mental health and counselling services, academic support center, and student financial hardship fund — knowing these resources before you need them reduces the risk of a crisis escalating

MoHE_Scholarship_Office.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education — Scholarship Department

links.title: US I-94 Verification
links.description: Check your official US entry record and admission class after arrival

links.title: Omani Ministry of Higher Education
links.description: Submit enrollment confirmations and bank account registration

forms: MoHE Bank Account Registration Form (submit before departure or immediately on arrival)
Initial Enrollment Confirmation Letter (from your university registrar — within 60 days of arrival)
Arrival Notification Email to MoHE (within 7 days of arrival)
Omani Embassy Scholar Registration (at your host-country embassy within first week)

complianceNotes: The most common first-semester mistake is delaying bank account registration — your stipend cannot be paid until this is done; do it in your first week
Embassy registration and MoHE reporting are separate obligations — completing one does not satisfy the other

If anything goes wrong in your first 30 days (visa discrepancy, enrollment issue, bank delay), contact both MoHE and the Omani embassy in your host country immediately — early escalation resolves faster than late disclosure

documents.title: MoHE Marriage and Dependent Policies
documents.summary: MoHE has specific rules governing marriage during your scholarship period and the joining of family members (spouse and children) in your host country. These are not purely personal decisions — they have scholarship, financial, and visa implications. Marriage must be reported to MoHE. Dependents joining you abroad requires prior MoHE notification and may affect your financial entitlements. This document covers reporting obligations, financial arrangements for dependents, and visa implications by destination.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients — US, UK, and Australia
scope.failureImpact: Unreported marriage or undisclosed dependents can constitute a breach of your scholarship agreement; dependent visa status issues can affect the scholar's own visa

details: Marriage during your scholarship period must be reported to MoHE in writing within 30 days of the marriage date — include a copy of your marriage certificate
Marriage does not automatically entitle you to additional financial support or a change in scholarship terms — any request for additional allowance must be made separately

Marriage in your host country is legally valid in Oman only if conducted in accordance with Omani personal status law or recognized under an agreement between Oman and the host country — consult the Omani embassy in your host country for guidance on recognition procedures before you marry abroad
If your spouse is a non-Omani national, additional documentation may be required by MoHE for scholarship record purposes — confirm requirements with MoHE Scholarship Department
Marriage does not suspend, extend, or otherwise modify your scholarship obligations — your GPA, enrollment, and reporting requirements remain unchanged
authorizationSources: Omani Embassy in your host country
controls: Report your marriage to MoHE and the Omani embassy in your host country within 30 days
Obtain an authenticated copy of your marriage certificate from the Omani embassy for your personal records

details: You must notify MoHE in writing before your spouse or children join you in your host country — this is a disclosure obligation, not an approval process in most cases, but the timing matters
MoHE does not typically pay for dependent living costs under a standard scholarship agreement — confirm whether your specific agreement includes a dependent allowance; do not assume one exists

If your agreement includes a dependent allowance, it is typically a fixed supplement per dependent and is only activated upon written application with supporting documentation (marriage certificate, birth certificates, dependent visa confirmation)
Dependents must hold the appropriate visa for your host country — they cannot enter or remain in the country on a tourist visa for the duration of your studies (see destination-specific notes below)

Your dependent's visa status is linked to your student visa in some destinations — a problem with your student visa can affect their right to remain; conversely, a dependent visa overstay can create complications for your own compliance record
controls: Apply for any dependent allowance before your dependents travel — do not assume payment will be backdated
Keep copies of all dependent visa approvals on file alongside your own scholarship documents

details: Spouses and unmarried children under 21 of F-1 students may apply for F-2 dependent visas
F-2 holders may NOT work in the United States under any circumstances — this is a hard US immigration rule
F-2 holders may study part-time but may not pursue a full course of study at a US institution without changing to a different visa status
Your DSO must issue an updated I-20 listing your dependent before they apply for the F-2 visa — contact your DSO as your first step
F-2 status is tied to your F-1 status — if your F-1 is terminated or you transfer institutions, your dependents' F-2 status is also affected immediately
You may bring dependants (spouse/civil partner and children under 18) to the UK if you are a postgraduate student on a course lasting more than 9 months, or an eligible government-sponsored student
Undergraduate students and students on courses of 9 months or less generally cannot bring dependants to the UK under the Student route — this is a UK Home Office restriction, not just an MoHE rule
Student Dependant visa holders may work in the UK if the main student has permission to work — check your Student visa Biometric Residence Permit (BRP) for your work permission
Your dependant's right to remain in the UK is contingent on your continuing student status — if you withdraw, transfer, or your visa is curtailed, their visa is affected
Apply for Student Dependant visas at the same time as or after your own Student visa — they cannot be granted before yours
authorizationSources: Your institution's International Student Advisory Service

rules.rule: Dependent Visa — Australia (Student Visa 500 — Secondary Applicant)
details: Spouses and dependent children can be included as secondary applicants on your Australian Student visa (subclass 500) or apply for their own Student visa as secondary holders
Secondary applicants must be listed before or at the time of your visa grant — adding a dependent after your visa has been granted requires a separate visa application

Work rights for secondary applicants: spouses of student visa holders in Australia generally have unlimited work rights during your studies (as of the time of writing — confirm current rules with the Department of Home Affairs as policy changes)
Dependent children may attend school in Australia — OSHC does not automatically cover dependants; you must enroll them in a separate Overseas Visitor Health Cover (OVHC) or equivalent policy
Contact MoHE to confirm whether OSHC for dependants is a covered entitlement under your scholarship agreement or a personal cost

If MoHE does not cover dependent costs, you must demonstrate sufficient funds to support your dependants in your visa application — ensure you can evidence this without relying on anticipated scholarship funds that are not confirmed in writing
The cost of living for a spouse and child significantly exceeds a typical scholarship stipend in all three destinations — make a realistic monthly budget before your dependants travel
Dependent school fees in Australia are significant for international students — public school fees for temporary residents apply in most states; this is typically not covered by MoHE
UK NHS surcharge must be paid for each Student Dependant visa applicant — this is an upfront cost at the time of application, not covered by the NHS once in the UK

forms: MoHE Marriage Notification (submit within 30 days of marriage with marriage certificate)
Dependent Allowance Application Form (if applicable under your scholarship agreement)
I-20 Update Request for F-2 Dependent (US — submit to your DSO first)
Student Dependant Visa Application (UK — via UKVI online portal)

complianceNotes: Report your marriage to MoHE regardless of whether you intend to bring your spouse abroad — unreported life changes create compliance gaps
Dependent visa rules differ significantly across destinations — read the destination-specific rules carefully and do not assume what applies in one country applies in another
A dependent's immigration problem is your compliance problem — their visa overstay or unauthorized work can affect your scholarship record; monitor their status as carefully as your own

documents.title: MoHE and Omani Embassy Contacts by Country
documents.summary: Key contact information for Omani government and scholarship support offices across all three study destinations — United States, United Kingdom, and Australia. The Cultural Attaché offices are the primary in-country contact for day-to-day scholarship support. The MoHE Scholarship Department in Muscat is the final authority on all scholarship decisions. In any emergency, contact both simultaneously.

appliesTo: All MoHE scholarship recipients in the US, UK, and Australia

MoHE_Muscat.name: Oman Ministry of Higher Education — Scholarship Department
MoHE_Muscat.office: Al Khuwair, Muscat, Sultanate of Oman
MoHE_Muscat.description: Primary authority for all scholarship decisions: program changes, extensions, leave, financial entitlements, and post-graduation return obligations

Cultural_Attache_US.name: Cultural Attaché Office — Embassy of Oman, Washington D.C.
Cultural_Attache_US.title: Student Affairs Division
Cultural_Attache_US.office: 2535 Belmont Road NW, Washington, D.C. 20008, USA
Cultural_Attache_US.description: First in-country contact for Omani scholars in the United States — emergency support, consular assistance, and escalation to Muscat

Embassy_UK.name: Embassy of Oman — London
Embassy_UK.office: 167 Queens Gate, London SW7 5HE, United Kingdom
Embassy_UK.phone_note: Current direct phone and student affairs email not verified. Use the official embassy website or email for current contact details before saving to your phone.
Embassy_UK.description: Omani embassy serving scholars in the United Kingdom — consular services, passport renewal, and student affairs via the educational or cultural attaché section

Embassy_Australia.name: Embassy of Oman — Canberra
Embassy_Australia.phone_note: Current direct phone and student affairs email not verified. Use the official embassy website to obtain current contact details before saving to your phone.
Embassy_Australia.description: Omani embassy serving scholars in Australia — consular services, passport renewal, and escalation of urgent scholarship matters to MoHE Muscat

rules.rule: When to Contact the Embassy vs. MoHE Muscat
details: Contact the Cultural Attaché or Embassy in your host country first for: immediate emergencies (arrest, hospitalization, theft, lost passport), consular services (passport renewal, emergency travel document), in-country navigation of local government systems, and urgent escalations that cannot wait for Muscat office hours

Contact MoHE Muscat (scholarships@mohe.gov.om) for: all formal scholarship decisions (program changes, extensions, leave approvals, financial claims, reimbursements), academic reporting submissions, and return-obligation planning
In a genuine emergency, contact both simultaneously — do not wait for one to respond before reaching out to the other
The Cultural Attaché supports and advocates for you but does not have final decision authority over scholarship matters — all formal approvals must come from MoHE Muscat
Life-threatening emergency: call local emergency services FIRST (US: 911 / UK: 999 / AU: 000), then notify the Omani embassy

Arrest or legal issue: do not make any statements to police beyond confirming your identity; contact the Omani embassy immediately and request consular access — embassies can dispatch a consular officer to assist
Lost or stolen passport: report to local police first to obtain a crime reference number, then contact the Omani embassy to arrange an emergency travel document

Hospitalization: if you are conscious, contact both the Omani embassy and your university's international student emergency line; if you are not, ensure your emergency contact — provided to MoHE and your university on arrival — can notify both
In all serious incidents: notify your university's international student office AND the Omani embassy on the same day
Save all embassy phone numbers and email addresses to your phone before you travel — do not rely on finding them in an emergency
Verify current contact details directly on the official embassy websites before saving — contact information changes
Share your emergency contact details with a trusted person in both Oman and your host country
Know the address of the Omani embassy in your host city — you may need to visit in person for passport renewal or consular assistance

complianceNotes: Save emergency contacts before you travel — never rely on finding them at the moment you need them
Verify all phone numbers and emails on the official embassy websites before storing them; contact details are updated periodically
Your university's International Student Office is an additional first-responder for non-consular emergencies — know their emergency line as well
